message:
chore(beads): update issue metadata from concurrent agent sessions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1472,7 +1472,11 @@
 {"id":"bd-db300.9","title":"Track I: revised structural convoy elimination with leases, leaf locality, and micro-publish","description":"## Goal
Eliminate the accidental shared surfaces that make logically disjoint concurrent inserts collapse.

## Background
The benchmark matrix and profiling work now support a stronger thesis than the earlier plan: FrankenSQLite should stop treating parent/internal/header/allocation state as ordinary long-lived transaction-owned pages. Shared structure must either disappear from the common path or shrink to tiny publish windows. The failed preclaim experiments matter because they falsified earlier-claim and longer-hold approaches.

## Scope
This track covers:
- the minimal truthing pass needed to stop guessing
- allocator and page-1/freelist decoupling via leases and deltas
- keeping concurrent inserts leaf-local longer
- version-gated parent micro-publish and local structural restart
- write-path tiering and restart-scope reduction
- explicit verification with targeted unit tests, e2e scripts, rich logging, and focused matrix reruns

## Guardrails
- never lengthen shared ownership without hard benchmark proof
- never regress to single-writer serialization
- preserve correctness and SQLite-file compatibility","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the track covers the full revised structural program from truthing through allocator decoupling, leaf-locality policy, micro-publish, hot-path tiering, and verification
- each child bead is self-contained enough that a future agent can implement it without reopening the original markdown plan
- the track explicitly includes unit tests, e2e checks, and structured logging obligations for every implementation phase
- the track avoids the already-falsified preclaim family of ideas","notes":"Mapping note:
- Phase 0 truthing -> I1, I2, I3 plus J1 for the baseline-cache truthing leg
- Phase 1 allocator/header decoupling -> I4, I5
- Phase 2 keep inserts leaf-local longer -> I7, I8
- Phase 3 micro-publish and structural intents -> I9, I10, I11
- Phase 4 tiered write path and cheap losing -> I6, I10, I12

Fundamental-vs-accidental shared-surface rule:
- fundamental shared surfaces: the truly hot target leaf when writers touch the same physical page, irreducible commit/publication ordering surfaces, and true serializability validation surfaces
- accidental shared surfaces to shrink or remove: page 1 on ordinary insert paths, freelist/header metadata on every allocation, parent/internal pages owned through full split computation, broad non-root rebalance on concurrent insert paths, centralized hot lookups on every write, and whole-transaction restart after a local structural loss

Explicit avoid list for this track:
- do not reintroduce broad structural preclaim, parent-only preclaim, universal blocking waits, blind "validate only at commit" OCC, or a first-wave full Bw-tree rewrite
- do not lengthen shared ownership without benchmark proof

Decision-gate reminder:
- if workload truthing shows the bad row is really right-edge append geometry, prioritize right-edge policy and append-aware routing sooner
- if allocator/page-1 conflict dominates, allocator decoupling outranks deeper tree work
- if parent-version churn still dominates after Phases 1-4, structural intents and later research branches move up

User-facing success condition:
- a future operator or agent should be able to explain, from this track alone, why disjoint logical inserts stopped collapsing, which accidental shared surfaces were removed or shrunk, which ideas are explicitly forbidden because they already failed in spirit, and which verification bundle proves the claim without reopening old sessions","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:22.766991823Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:01:56.896781723Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","mvcc","parallelism","perf","sqlite","structural-convoy","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9","depends_on_id":"bd-db300","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:22.766991823Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.1","title":"I1: instrument retry-cause taxonomy and per-transaction page-touch classes","description":"## Goal
Replace raw retry counts with exact retry causes and page-touch-class accounting for the worst rows.

## Why This Exists
The revised plan refuses to guess whether the dominant loss is true hot-leaf contention, parent/internal convoy, right-edge race, allocator/page-1 conflict, snapshot invalidation, or publication conflict. Raw retry counts are not enough.

## Scope
- emit stable retry-cause categories such as target-leaf-owned, parent-version-changed, parent-busy, right-edge-split-race, page-1/header-conflict, allocator-extent-refill-conflict, snapshot-invalidation, and commit/publication-conflict
- emit per-transaction page-touch histograms by class: table leaf, index leaf, internal, root, right-edge path, page-1/allocator metadata, and commit/publication metadata
- surface the new data in focused artifacts for the catastrophic disjoint row and at least one mixed row
- add structured logging that lets future matrix runs report these fields without ad hoc patching","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- focused bad-row and mixed-row artifacts contain exact retry-cause histograms and page-touch-class histograms
- artifact fields and log names are stable enough for future automated matrix reporting
- unit tests verify aggregation correctness and e2e checks prove the new fields appear in benchmark output","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:43.969219164Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:26:56.195585695Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","db300-program","db300-track-i","evidence","logging","measurement","retry-taxonomy","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:26:45.545303869Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.1.3.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:26:56.194445781Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:43.969219164Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.10","title":"I10: implement private split planning, short final parent publish, and local structural restart","description":"## Goal
Land the first concrete micro-publish implementation from I9.

## Scope
- perform expensive split planning before shared ownership
- use parent version tokens or equivalent light anchor validation
- own the parent only for the final publish delta
- on mismatch, restart from the local structural planning point rather than from the full transaction where correctness allows
- add targeted unit tests, concurrency e2e tests, and detailed logging for parent hold duration, mismatch counts, and local-restart counts","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- parent hold times shrink sharply relative to the pre-implementation baseline
- structural losses no longer force whole-transaction restart in the common mismatch case
- unit and e2e tests cover mismatch, restart, rollback, and publish correctness, and focused benchmark artifacts show real throughput and retry improvement","notes":"Implementation proof requirements:
- add unit tests for version-token mismatch, local structural restart, publish success, rollback after prepared split state, and crash-safe replay boundaries where applicable
- add contention-heavy e2e runs that capture parent_hold_duration, parent_version_mismatch_count, local_structural_restart_count, and whole_txn_restart_count
- require artifact packs that make the preclaim failure mode and the micro-publish improvement directly comparable","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:15.570729837Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:39:31.927337801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","hot-path","micro-publish","mvcc","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:15.570729837Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:30.146156122Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.10","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:40.446096194Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-db300.9.11","title":"I11: extend the structural path into explicit intents and document the later-stage research branches","description":"## Goal
Move beyond ordinary page ownership for parent/header work and capture the later-stage options in a controlled way.

## Scope
- define structural intent types such as parent separator insert, child pointer replacement, root promotion, header delta, and extent reservation delta
- describe how leaf work remains private while structural intents are merged or published late
- prototype the first intent path if the simpler micro-publish protocol still leaves parent convoy too dominant
- document, as lower-priority research branches rather than immediate work, the nested-top-action-like split path, runtime split sidecar/B-link-like assist, and internal-page delta-chain branch","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- structural intents are defined concretely enough to become implementation work when justified by evidence
- later-stage research branches are captured with explicit prerequisites and falsifiers rather than as vague ideas
- the bead makes clear that these branches are later-stage bets, not the next patch
- the bead records what evidence would justify promoting structural intents ahead of simpler micro-publish work in the future","notes":"Research-branch gatekeeping:
- structural intents are the next architectural step only if allocator decoupling, leaf-locality policy, write-path tiering, and first-wave micro-publish still leave parent convoy as the dominant bottleneck
- document explicit falsifiers for promoting later branches: nested-top-action-like physical splits, runtime split sidecar/B-link-like assist, and internal-page delta chains must each name the evidence that would justify them and the simpler branches that were already exhausted
- require design-level verification hooks up front if any branch advances: named intent classes, merge anchors, recovery invariants, logging schema, focused e2e scenarios, and artifact bundle shape
- keep this bead adversarial toward glamour ideas: it exists to prevent a premature jump to high-risk structural rewrites before the lower-blast-radius program has genuinely been tried and measured","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:26.027609526Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:02:51.208875611Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","intents","research","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:26.027609526Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:50.776208510Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.11","title":"I11: extend the structural path into explicit intents and document the later-stage research branches","description":"## Goal
Move beyond ordinary page ownership for parent/header work and capture the later-stage options in a controlled way.

## Scope
- define structural intent types such as parent separator insert, child pointer replacement, root promotion, header delta, and extent reservation delta
- describe how leaf work remains private while structural intents are merged or published late
- prototype the first intent path if the simpler micro-publish protocol still leaves parent convoy too dominant
- break out explicitly gated late-stage research branches for broader optimistic structural validation, nested-top-action-like physical operations, runtime B-link/Bw-tree-style assists, and any proposal that touches compatibility-sensitive on-disk bytes
- document these as lower-priority research branches rather than immediate work","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- structural intents are defined concretely enough to become implementation work when justified by evidence
- later-stage research branches are captured with explicit prerequisites and falsifiers rather than as vague ideas
- the bead makes clear that these branches are later-stage bets, not the next patch
- the bead records what evidence would justify promoting structural intents ahead of simpler micro-publish work in the future","notes":"Research-branch gatekeeping:
- structural intents are the next architectural step only if allocator decoupling, leaf-locality policy, write-path tiering, and first-wave micro-publish still leave parent convoy as the dominant bottleneck
- child research beads under I11 capture the explicitly-late branches: I11.1 broader optimistic validation, I11.2 nested-top-action-like physical operations, I11.3 runtime B-link/Bw-tree-style assists, and I11.4 byte-accurate compatibility-budget proof for any on-disk byte reuse
- document explicit falsifiers for promoting later branches: each branch must name the evidence that would justify it and the simpler branches that were already exhausted
- require design-level verification hooks up front if any branch advances: named intent classes, merge anchors, recovery invariants, logging schema, focused e2e scenarios, and artifact bundle shape
- keep this bead adversarial toward glamour ideas: it exists to prevent a premature jump to high-risk structural rewrites before the lower-blast-radius program has genuinely been tried and measured","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:26.027609526Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:31:57.908524958Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","intents","research","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:26.027609526Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:14:50.776208510Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.11.1","title":"I11.1: evaluate broader optimistic structural validation without regressing into blind pure-OCC everywhere","description":"## Goal
Preserve the literature-backed idea of broader optimistic structural validation as a late-stage option without letting it contaminate the first-wave correctness-preserving path.

## Scope
- evaluate whether any additional validation can move later in the structural/write path after allocator decoupling, leaf-locality policy, write-path tiering, and first-wave micro-publish have already been tried
- explicitly reject the naive day-one version of the idea: blind pure-OCC everywhere with correctness and wasted-work risk hand-waved away
- define the precise boundary between acceptable late validation and unacceptable blind deferral of essential checks
- record what evidence would justify even opening a prototype branch","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead defines a precise boundary between acceptable late structural validation and the rejected blind pure-OCC-everywhere design, including serializability, SSI/page-level conflict, and wasted-work implications.
2. The bead records explicit evidence thresholds that must be met before a prototype is even allowed, including proof that allocator decoupling, leaf-locality policy, write-path tiering, and first-wave micro-publish were insufficient.
3. The bead names the verification burden a prototype would owe: unit tests, contention-heavy e2e cases, structured logs, and artifact packs that can expose wasted-work explosions or correctness risk.
4. The result makes it easy to reject the branch cleanly if the evidence does not justify it.","notes":"Late-OCC gatekeeping:
- this bead exists to preserve the literature-backed idea without allowing a naive day-one jump to blind pure-OCC everywhere
- require a concrete risk ledger for serializable correctness, wasted-work amplification, retry storms, and proof burden versus expected upside
- any prototype must specify what checks remain early, what moves later, and why that does not compromise correctness or make losing too expensive
- optimize for users by ensuring this branch is only explored if it has a realistic chance of helping more than it harms","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:29:33.051667353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:32:05.629633653Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","db300-program","db300-track-i","mvcc","research","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:29:33.051667353Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.1","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:31:00.265004115Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.11.2","title":"I11.2: research nested-top-action-like autonomous physical split/install operations with a full recovery story","description":"## Goal
Capture ARIES-style nested-top-action-inspired physical split/install work as a legitimate but strictly later-stage branch.

## Scope
- investigate whether physical split/install work could survive user rollback while logical row visibility remains transactional
- require a crisp recovery, crash, rollback, and replay story before any prototype is considered
- treat the idea as evidence-gated architecture research, not as a shortcut around first-wave micro-publish and local-restart work
- record the proof burden that would be required before implementation is allowed","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead defines the recovery, crash, rollback, and replay story required before nested-top-action-like physical split/install work could ever be prototyped.
2. The bead states exactly which physical actions would survive user rollback, which logical effects remain transactional, and how correctness would be verified under crash/fault testing.
3. The bead records explicit reasons this branch remains later-stage and what evidence would justify opening it after simpler micro-publish/intents work.
4. The result makes it possible to reject the branch decisively if the recovery story or verification burden is not compelling.","notes":"Autonomous-physical-op gatekeeping:
- treat ARIES-style nested-top-action inspiration as legitimate but high-burden research, not as a shortcut around current correctness work
- require a byte- and event-level recovery narrative, crash/fault test plan, rollback semantics, and artifact/logging contract before any prototype is allowed
- compare the expected upside against first-wave micro-publish and structural-intent options instead of assuming autonomy is automatically better
- optimize for users by refusing to trade understandable recovery behavior for speculative throughput wins","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:29:51.466013954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:32:14.275767974Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","recovery","research","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:29:51.466013954Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:31:11.490410471Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.11.3","title":"I11.3: research runtime B-link/Bw-tree-style half-splits, side links, and delta-style internal updates without on-disk compatibility hacks","description":"## Goal
Preserve the legitimate B-link/Bw-tree family of ideas as late-stage options without jumping prematurely to a full structural rewrite or unsafe file-format tricks.

## Scope
- investigate runtime-only side-link or half-split assists, child-first routing aids, and delta-style internal-page updates as later options if simpler micro-publish and structural-intent work are still insufficient
- explicitly avoid assuming any on-disk byte budget or file-format slack without proof
- keep the branch focused on runtime metadata or otherwise compatibility-preserving mechanisms first
- record what evidence would justify moving from intent/micro-publish work into this family","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead defines one or more runtime-first B-link/Bw-tree-inspired options, such as half-splits, side-link assists, child-first routing aids, or delta-style internal updates, without assuming on-disk compatibility hacks.
2. The bead records the conditions under which this family becomes justified only after simpler micro-publish and structural-intent approaches remain insufficient.
3. The bead names the correctness, verification, and operator-observability burden a prototype would owe, including fallback behavior and evidence bundles.
4. The result keeps the branch available without letting it masquerade as a first-wave fix.","notes":"Runtime-side-structure gatekeeping:
- prioritize runtime-only or otherwise compatibility-preserving variants first; any move toward on-disk byte reuse must defer to I11.4
- require explicit comparison against simpler micro-publish/intents paths and against a full structural rewrite so the team can explain why this middle ground is worth the complexity
- record the observability contract a prototype would need, including routing-choice logs, fallback indicators, and before/after contention evidence
- optimize for users by preserving compatibility and debuggability ahead of clever but opaque routing tricks","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:30:11.233725087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:32:21.677592657Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","btree","db300-program","db300-track-i","mvcc","research","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:30:11.233725087Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:31:23.153813097Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-db300.9.11.4","title":"I11.4: prove byte-accurate compatibility budget before any proposal that reuses page-header or reserved-file bytes","description":"## Goal
Create an explicit stop-gate against casual file-format hacking by requiring byte-accurate compatibility proof before any side-link or metadata idea touches on-disk reserved space.

## Scope
- enumerate exactly which bytes, if any, could ever be considered for alternate use without violating SQLite-file compatibility
- define the proof burden for compatibility, crash safety, tooling compatibility, and legacy-reader behavior
- make "unused page-header bytes" or similar claims impossible to smuggle into implementation without this gate being cleared
- keep the result reusable by any future side-link or structural-metadata research bead","acceptance_criteria":"1. The bead enumerates the exact on-disk byte budget relevant to any proposed side-link or metadata scheme and states clearly whether any safe compatibility budget exists at all.
2. The bead defines the proof burden for SQLite-file compatibility, crash safety, tooling interoperability, and legacy-reader behavior before any reserved-byte or page-header-byte reuse is allowed.
3. The bead records a clear no-go outcome if the byte budget or compatibility proof is insufficient, rather than leaving the question permanently ambiguous.
4. The result becomes the mandatory gate for any future branch that proposes touching reserved or header bytes.","notes":"Compatibility-budget gatekeeping:
- treat claims about "unused bytes" as untrusted until proven byte-by-byte against the real file format and compatibility envelope
- require an explicit map of candidate bytes, constraints on their use, crash/recovery implications, and tooling/legacy-reader implications
- allow the correct outcome to be a permanent prohibition rather than forcing the project to keep the idea alive artificially
- optimize for users by preventing compatibility regressions from being smuggled in under the banner of performance experimentation","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:30:28.885886816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-15T00:32:32.137032272Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["architecture","compatibility","db300-program","db300-track-i","research","sqlite","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.11","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:30:28.885886816Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.11.4","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.12","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-15T00:31:37.522629995Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.12","title":"I12: verify the revised structural track with targeted unit tests, e2e scripts, rich logs, and focused matrix attribution","description":"## Goal
Make the revised structural program falsifiable and reproducible.

## Scope
- map every structural-track implementation bead to concrete unit tests, e2e scripts, and structured log fields
- require replayable benchmark commands and before/after focused artifacts for allocator/page-1, right-edge, parent-hold, retry-cause, and restart-scope changes
- prove that improvements are attributable to specific structural levers rather than noise or a new correctness bug
- feed the result into the canonical matrix comparison and the scorecard/reporting track without losing negative results or neutral outcomes
- require exact operator-facing rerun entrypoints and failure triage steps for each structural validation bundle","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- every structural implementation bead has named unit tests, named e2e scripts, expected artifact paths, and required structured log event names recorded
- the verification plan includes negative-path coverage such as lease exhaustion, parent version mismatch, restart-scope fallback, and rollback/recovery cases where relevant
- focused benchmark artifacts and attribution notes exist for the major structural levers
- the verification bundle is good enough that a future agent can tell which structural ideas worked, failed, or remain unproven without reopening old sessions","notes":"Verification bundle requirements:
- for each structural implementation bead, record the exact unit/property test modules or scenario families, the required e2e harness/script entrypoints, the expected structured log event families, and the artifact bundle keys or directories
- require before/after focused reruns for allocator/page-1, right-edge policy, write-tiering, parent micro-publish, and local-restart behavior
- preserve negative and neutral outcomes in the same bundle shape so failed ideas remain legible instead of disappearing from the record

Closure standard:
- a future agent should be able to rerun the full structural proof set from this bead and tell which ideas helped, which hurt, and which remain unresolved without reopening planning documents","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:36.812921793Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:39:29.363341704Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-i","e2e","logging","track-i","unit-tests","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.5.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:28:01.264653162Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.6.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:33:27.399105617Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.7.7.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:33:38.584396898Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:09:36.812921793Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.10","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:15:32.510911686Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:15:01.087381478Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:15:11.510803525Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.12","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:15:21.982912276Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.2","title":"I2: instrument split-path taxonomy, hold/wait distributions, copy-allocation churn, and cache-hit truthing","description":"## Goal
Measure the second layer of truthing data needed to separate structural convoy from baseline waste.

## Why This Exists
The revised plan needs to know not only why retries happen, but also how often inserts use local insert vs local split vs right-edge split vs broad rebalance; how long shared surfaces are held; how much copy/allocation churn occurs; and whether statement/record/page-buffer caches are actually hitting.

## Scope
- count split-path usage: pure leaf-local insert, local leaf split, right-edge split, full non-root rebalance, root split
- capture median and p99 hold/wait duration for parent/internal surfaces, allocator/page-1 surfaces, and structural waits per successful transaction
- record staged full-page copies, bytes copied, heap allocations, PageData ownership conversions, and record decodes per transaction on representative rows
- measure statement-plan cache hit rate, cursor reuse rate, decode-cache hit rate, and page-buffer pool hit rate where those facilities exist","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- representative rows expose split-path counts, hold/wait distributions, copy/allocation counters, and cache-hit metrics in stable artifacts
- unit tests cover counter aggregation and edge cases such as zero-hit rows or no-wait rows
- the instrumentation is lightweight enough that focused benchmark reruns remain usable for optimization steering","notes":"Coordination note:
I2 owns the structural-side truthing, but it should share counters and artifact layout with J1 rather than blocking the baseline-tax truthing lane. The design intent is parallel measurement after the minimal A3 leafs unlock.

Verification/logging requirement:
- require a benchmark-output smoke check proving these fields appear on the focused bad row and at least one mixed row
- record stable structured log families for split_path_summary, hold_wait_distribution, copy_alloc_churn, and cache_hit_ledger so later implementation beads can cite them directly","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:53.900701018Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:39:28.505622365Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["allocation","cache","copy-reduction","db300-program","db300-track-i","evidence","measurement","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:07:53.900701018Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.2","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:06.181574737Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-db300.9.3","title":"I3: truth workload semantics for disjoint inserts and publish the revised conflict geometry note","description":"## Goal
Stop trusting benchmark labels and explicitly document what the worst workloads actually mean physically.

## Scope
- establish whether keys and rowids are monotone, whether secondary indexes are touched, and whether inserts converge on the same right-most leaf or parent chain
- tie benchmark SQL shape to actual page-space conflict surfaces using I1 and I2 data
- write the conflict-geometry note that future agents can consult instead of re-deriving the workload shape from code
- explicitly call out which conflicts are fundamental versus accidental for the focused bad row and one mixed row","acceptance_criteria":"Success means:
- the disjoint-insert workload has an explicit physical conflict description that future agents can cite directly from the bead
- the note distinguishes true hot-leaf contention, right-edge growth, parent/internal convoy, allocator/page-1 false sharing, and publication surfaces
- at least one mixed row receives the same treatment so the structural story is not overfit to a single benchmark family
- the deliverable includes a compact SQL-shape summary, a page-touch-class summary, and a clear prioritization takeaway for later structural beads","notes":"Deliverable requirement:
- close this bead with a compact, self-contained conflict-geometry packet: workload SQL/transaction summary, physical page-touch summary, retry-cause summary, and a short statement of what that means for prioritization
- the output should be legible to someone who has not read the benchmark code recently and should directly answer whether the row is truly disjoint, append-heavy, parent-convoy-heavy, allocator-heavy, or mixed","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:03.923933112Z","created_by":"codex","updated_at":"2026-03-14T23:55:25.006349297Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["benchmark","db300-program","db300-track-i","evidence","realdb","right-edge","track-i"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:08:03.923933112Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:27.027715310Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-db300.9.3","depends_on_id":"bd-db300.9.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-14T23:12:37.261247514Z","created_by":"codex","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
